speaker: LAFEW
text: But I hope your Lordship thinks not him a

speaker: LAFEW
text: soldier.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Yes, my lord, and of very valiant approof.

speaker: LAFEW
text: You have it from his own deliverance.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: And by other warranted testimony.

speaker: LAFEW
text: Then my dial goes not true. I took this lark for

speaker: LAFEW
text: a bunting.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I do assure you, my lord, he is very great in

speaker: BERTRAM
text: knowledge and accordingly valiant.

speaker: LAFEW
text: I have then sinned against his experience and

speaker: LAFEW
text: transgressed against his valor, and my state that

speaker: LAFEW
text: way is dangerous since I cannot yet find in my

speaker: LAFEW
text: heart to repent. Here he comes. I pray you make us

speaker: LAFEW
text: friends. I will pursue the amity.

speaker: PAROLLES
text: These things shall be done, sir.

speaker: LAFEW
text: Pray you, sir, who’s his tailor?

speaker: PAROLLES
text: Sir?

speaker: LAFEW
text: O, I know him well. Ay, sir, he, sir, ’s a good

speaker: LAFEW
text: workman, a very good tailor.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Is she gone to the King?

speaker: PAROLLES
text: She is.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Will she away tonight?

speaker: PAROLLES
text: As you’ll have her.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I have writ my letters, casketed my treasure,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Given order for our horses, and tonight,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: When I should take possession of the bride,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: End ere I do begin.

speaker: LAFEW
text: A good traveler is something at the latter

speaker: LAFEW
text: end of a dinner, but one that lies three thirds,

speaker: LAFEW
text: and uses a known truth to pass a thousand nothings

speaker: LAFEW
text: with, should be once heard and thrice beaten.—

speaker: LAFEW
text: God save you, captain.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Is there any unkindness

speaker: BERTRAM
text: between my lord and you, monsieur?

speaker: PAROLLES
text: I know not how I have deserved to run into

speaker: PAROLLES
text: my lord’s displeasure.

speaker: LAFEW
text: You have made shift to run into ’t, boots and

speaker: LAFEW
text: spurs and all, like him that leapt into the custard;

speaker: LAFEW
text: and out of it you’ll run again rather than suffer

speaker: LAFEW
text: question for your residence.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: It may be you have mistaken him, my lord.

speaker: LAFEW
text: And shall do so ever, though I took him at ’s

speaker: LAFEW
text: prayers. Fare you well, my lord, and believe this of

speaker: LAFEW
text: me: there can be no kernel in this light nut. The

speaker: LAFEW
text: soul of this man is his clothes. Trust him not in

speaker: LAFEW
text: matter of heavy consequence. I have kept of them

speaker: LAFEW
text: tame and know their natures.—Farewell, monsieur.

speaker: LAFEW
text: I have spoken better of you than you have or

speaker: LAFEW
text: will to deserve at my hand, but we must do good

speaker: LAFEW
text: against evil.

speaker: PAROLLES
text: An idle lord, I swear.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I think not so.

speaker: PAROLLES
text: Why, do you not know him?

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Yes, I do know him well, and common speech

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Gives him a worthy pass.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Here comes my clog.

speaker: HELEN
text: I have, sir, as I was commanded from you,

speaker: HELEN
text: Spoke with the King and have procured his leave

speaker: HELEN
text: For present parting. Only he desires

speaker: HELEN
text: Some private speech with you.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I shall obey his will.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: You must not marvel, Helen, at my course,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Which holds not color with the time, nor does

speaker: BERTRAM
text: The ministration and requirèd office

speaker: BERTRAM
text: On my particular. Prepared I was not

speaker: BERTRAM
text: For such a business; therefore am I found

speaker: BERTRAM
text: So much unsettled. This drives me to entreat you

speaker: BERTRAM
text: That presently you take your way for home,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: And rather muse than ask why I entreat you;

speaker: BERTRAM
text: For my respects are better than they seem,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: And my appointments have in them a need

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Greater than shows itself at the first view

speaker: BERTRAM
text: To you that know them not.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: This to my mother.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: ’Twill be two days ere I shall see you, so

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I leave you to your wisdom.

speaker: HELEN
text: Sir, I can nothing say

speaker: HELEN
text: But that I am your most obedient servant—

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Come, come, no more of that.

speaker: HELEN
text: And ever shall

speaker: HELEN
text: With true observance seek to eke out that

speaker: HELEN
text: Wherein toward me my homely stars have failed

speaker: HELEN
text: To equal my great fortune.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Let that go.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: My haste is very great. Farewell. Hie home.

speaker: HELEN
text: Pray, sir, your pardon.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Well, what would you say?

speaker: HELEN
text: I am not worthy of the wealth I owe,

speaker: HELEN
text: Nor dare I say ’tis mine—and yet it is—

speaker: HELEN
text: But, like a timorous thief, most fain would steal

speaker: HELEN
text: What law does vouch mine own.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: What would you have?

speaker: HELEN
text: Something, and scarce so much; nothing, indeed.

speaker: HELEN
text: I would not tell you what I would, my lord. Faith, yes:

speaker: HELEN
text: Strangers and foes do sunder and not kiss.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I pray you stay not, but in haste to horse.

speaker: HELEN
text: I shall not break your bidding, good my lord.—

speaker: HELEN
text: Where are my other men?—Monsieur, farewell.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Go thou toward home, where I will never come

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Whilst I can shake my sword or hear the drum.—

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Away, and for our flight.

speaker: PAROLLES
text: Bravely, coraggio!
